records.ety: [OE. welcome, welcume, wilcume, AS. wilcuma a welcome guest, from wil-, as a prefix, akin to willa will + cuma a comer, fr. cuman to come; hence, properly, one who comes so as to please another's will; cf. Icel. velkominn welcome, G. willkommen. See Will, n., and Come.]

defs: Received with gladness; admitted willingly to the house, entertainment, or company; as, a welcome visitor.
quotes: When the glad soul is made Heaven's welcome guest.

defs: Producing gladness; grateful; as, a welcome present; welcome news.
Free to have or enjoy gratuitously; as, you are welcome to the use of my library.
notes: ☞ Welcome is used elliptically for you are welcome. “Welcome, great monarch, to your own.”

defs: Kind reception of a guest or newcomer; as, we entered the house and found a ready welcome.
quotes: His warmest welcome at an inn.
Truth finds an entrance and a welcome too.
To thee and thy company I bid A hearty welcome.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Welcomed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Welcoming.]

defs: To salute with kindness, as a newcomer; to receive and entertain hospitably and cheerfully; as, to welcome a visitor; to welcome a new idea.
quotes: Thus we salute thee with our early song, And welcome thee, and wish thee long.
